revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24608848913|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:聖神社]] { {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Hijiri Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka) haiden+honden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall (rear right) and Worship Hall | 所在地 = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Izumi, Osaka|en|Izumi, Osaka|qid=Q696412|lt=Izumi City|1=Izumi, Osaka}} Ōji-cho 919 | 位置 = {{Wiki Coordinates DMS|34|30|04.4|N|135|26|49.0|E|scale:20000_region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Hijiri Shrine (Izumi City)}} | 祭神 = {{ill|Sacred Great Deity|ja|聖大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Great Deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524506|1=Hijiri no Ōkami}} | 社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}}{{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|qid=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
Old {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | 創建 = Tradition: {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11580612}} 3rd year ({{ill|675|en|675|qid=Q30523|lt=675 AD|1=675}}) | 本殿 = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|qid=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} | 別名 = Shinoda Myōjin | 札所等 = | 例祭 = {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|qid=Q2557|lt=May 10|1=May 10}} | 神事 = Government Festival, Sumo Festival }} '''Hijiri Shrine''' (聖神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōji-cho, {{ill|Izumi, Osaka|en|Izumi, Osaka|qid=Q696412|lt=Izumi City|1=Izumi, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Shrine Rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. It belongs to the {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}} and the {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|qid=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, and is also known as “Shinoda Myōjin”, “Shinoda Shrine”, and “Shinoda Seishinja” among other names. == 祭神 == * {{ill|Sacred Great Deity|ja|聖大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Great Deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524506|1=Hijiri no Ōkami}} (ひじりのおおかみ) {{ill|Sacred Great Deity|ja|聖大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Great Deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524506|1=Hijiri no Ōkami}} appears in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} as the child deity ({{ill|Child Deity|ja|御子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Child Deity|1=Mikogami|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}) of the {{ill|Great Age Deity|ja|大年神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Age Deity|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 年神|1=Toshigami|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}. According to the “Origin of the Great Deities of the Five Shrines of Izumi Province and the Main Shrine of Fuchū” the {{ill|Honji suijaku|en|Honji suijaku|qid=Q3080781|lt=Honji Buddha|1=Honji suijaku}} is regarded as {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|qid=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bodhisattva|1=Kṣitigarbha}}. * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Nigi-hayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt=Nigi-hayahi no Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi-hayahi|1=Nigihayahi|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohananosakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} * {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|qid=Q8191705|lt=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto|1=Iwanaga-hime}} == History == [[File:Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka) honden2.jpg|180px|thumb|Main Hall]] In the tradition of this shrine, there are two legends. One, noted in a ’s eastern expedition, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} was enshrined here.|date=May 2023}}, and the other, which states that on August 15 of the 3rd year of {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11580612}} ({{ill|675|en|675|qid=Q30523|lt=675 AD|1=675}}), by imperial command of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, Shinoda leader (しのだのおびと) of the immigrant ({{ill|Toraijin|en|Toraijin|qid=Q11561926|lt=Immigrant|1=Toraijin}}) clan ({{ill|Clan|en|Clan|qid=Q211503|lt=Clan|1=Clan}}) enshrined the {{ill|Sacred Great Deity|ja|聖大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Great Deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524506|1=Hijiri no Ōkami}}. On May 7 of the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} era ({{ill|859|en|859|qid=Q23897|lt=859 AD|1=859}}), it was designated as a {{ill|Government Shrine|ja|官社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官幣社|1=kokuheisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452104}}, and on August 13 of the same year, it received the shrine Rank ({{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|qid=Q11591025|lt=Shrine Rank|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}) from the 5th Rank below to the 4th Rank below (as recorded in the {{ill|Sandaijitsuroku|ja|三代実録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sandaijitsuroku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三代実録|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}). In the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (日本)|lt=Enchō|lt_ja=延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延長 (元号)|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1327274}} ({{ill|927|en|927|qid=Q30470|lt=927 AD|1=927}}), the “Divine Name Register” ({{ill|Engishiki Divine Name Register|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Divine Name Register|lt_ja=神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Divine Name Register|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}) compiled under the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} lists it ({{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}). In the medieval “Izumi Province Divine Name Register” ({{ill|Izumi Province Divine Name Register|ja|国内神名帳|lt=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|lt_ja=和泉国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Province Divine Name Register|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420490}}), the shrine Rank is recorded as “First Rank.” During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the Shinoda clan served as hereditary priests, but in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|qid=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, after siding with the {{ill|Southern Court|en|Southern Court|qid=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court|1=Southern Court}}, they lost their priestly status; and in the 5th year of {{ill|Enbun|en|Enbun|qid=Q1145071|lt=Enbun|1=Enbun}} ({{ill|1360|en|1360|qid=Q6150|lt=1360 AD|1=1360}}) the position was taken over by Wada Kurando. From the Nanboku-chō period onward, along with other shrines in {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}} such as {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|qid=Q705151|lt=Ōtori Taisha|1=Ōtori taisha}}, {{ill|Izumanaishi Shrine|ja|泉穴師神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumanaishi Shrine|1=Izumianashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11554908}}, {{ill|Tsugikawa Shrine|ja|積川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugikawa Shrine|1=Tsugawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597137}}, and {{ill|Hine Shrine|ja|日根神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine Shrine|1=Hine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11509543}}, it came to be known as one of the “Five Great Shrines.” Although it occupies the third Rank of shrines in Izumi Province, the term “Sannomiya” does not appear in medieval records. In the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|1575|en|1575|qid=Q6688|lt=1575 AD|1=1575}}), it was granted 1,100 {{ill|Koku|en|Koku|qid=Q855048|lt=stone|1=Koku}} as its domain by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, but in the 13th year ({{ill|1585|en|1585|qid=Q6736|lt=1585 AD|1=1585}}) during {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}’s {{ill|Kishū Expedition|ja|紀州征伐#和泉の戦い|lt=Negoro Attack|lt_ja=根来攻め|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Expedition|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10855721}}, the shrine suffered damage from warfare and its holdings were confiscated. In the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Toyotomi Hideyori appointed {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|en|Katagiri Katsumoto|qid=Q1882775|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}} as commissioner to construct and repair shrines and temples throughout the region, and this shrine was rebuilt in the 9th year of {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} ({{ill|1604|en|1604|qid=Q6738|lt=1604 AD|1=1604}}). The existing structures from that time—the main hall and the sub-shrines of the three shrines, the waterfall shrine, and the Hiraoka shrine—have been designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} and as {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q2901860|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} of {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. In addition, there was a {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|qid=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji|1=Jingū-ji}} affiliated with {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}}’s Manshōin, which featured a {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata}} and a Bell Hall (鐘堂). In the 8th year of {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|qid=Q1195131|lt=Kansei|1=Kansei}} ({{ill|1796|en|1796|qid=Q7818|lt=1796 AD|1=1796}}), a dispute arose between the shrine family ({{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}}) and the shrine priest ({{ill|Shrine Priest|ja|社僧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Priest|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別当寺|1=bettō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}). With the advent of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}) led to the closure of the Manshōin at the Jingū-ji. In {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=1872 AD|1=1872}} (5th year of Meiji), it was Ranked as a local shrine, and in March {{ill|1943|en|1943|qid=Q18623|lt=1943 AD|1=1943}} (18th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}}) it was elevated to {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|qid=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} status. The “Chū Izumi Kaidō”, which stretches from Yosumatsu-cho in {{ill|Izumiōtsu|en|Izumiōtsu|qid=Q873553|lt=Izumiotsu City|1=Izumiōtsu}}, is also known as the “Nunobiki Road”. It is said that when Shinoda Daimyōjin landed at Yosumatsu-hama, he drew cloth as he proceeded toward Hijiri Shrine. A monument commemorating the “Chū Izumi Kaidō” stands at its intersection with the {{ill|Kishū Kaidō|ja|紀州街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Kaidō|1=Kishū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605050}}. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka) honden1.jpg|180px|thumb|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)]] [[File:Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka) Taki-jinja+San-jinja.jpg|180px|thumb|Sub-shrine of the Three Shrines (right) and the Waterfall Shrine (left); both are Important Cultural Properties]] [[File:Hijiri-jinja (Izumi, Osaka) Hiraoka-jinja honden.jpg|180px|thumb|Main Hall of the Hiraoka Shrine (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Osaka Prefecture)]] * Main Hall ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) – Rebuilt in the 9th year of {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} ({{ill|1604|en|1604|qid=Q6738|lt=1604 AD|1=1604}}) by Toyotomi Hideyori with {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|en|Katagiri Katsumoto|qid=Q1882775|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}} as commissioner. The shrine structure is built in the {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|qid=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} style with {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress Bark Roofing|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q90373477}}, featuring a front decoration of {{ill|Chidori-hafū|ja|千鳥破風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chidori-hafū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 破風|1=Bargeboard|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4881511}} and a one-kan eave of {{ill|Karahafu|en|Karahafu|qid=Q977293|lt=Karahafū|1=Karahafu}} on the {{ill|Mukaehai|ja|向拝|1=kōhai|2=ja|3=向拝|4=zh|5=向拜|lt=kōhai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaehai|13=User:Immanuelle/kōhai (architecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11415970}}. It was designated a Specially Protected Building on April 15, {{ill|1924|en|1924|qid=Q2173|lt=1924 AD|1=1924}} (13th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}}) under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law|ja|古社寺保存法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law|1=Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11411552}}, and became an Important Cultural Property with the enforcement of the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} in {{ill|1950|en|1950|qid=Q18597|lt=1950 AD|1=1950}}. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} – A remnant of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu Shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. * Remote Worship Site – A modern structure modeled after the remote worship site of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. === Auxiliary and Subshrines === * Three Shrines (Important Cultural Property) – Rebuilt in the 9th year of {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} ({{ill|1604|en|1604|qid=Q6738|lt=1604 AD|1=1604}}) by Toyotomi Hideyori with {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|en|Katagiri Katsumoto|qid=Q1882775|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}} as commissioner. Its brilliant colors and ornate decorations exemplify the characteristics of the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安土桃山時代|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q319531}}. The shrine structure is built in the three-kan style in the {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} with Cypress Bark Roofing; the three-kan style (with three bays at the front) of the Kasuga-zukuri is rare nationwide. It was relocated to its present site in {{ill|1873|en|1873|qid=Q7757|lt=1873 AD|1=1873}} (6th year of Meiji). * Waterfall Shrine (Important Cultural Property) – Also rebuilt in the 9th year of {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} ({{ill|1604|en|1604|qid=Q6738|lt=1604 AD|1=1604}}) by Toyotomi Hideyori with {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|en|Katagiri Katsumoto|qid=Q1882775|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}} as commissioner. The shrine structure is a one-kan building in the Kasuga-zukuri style with Cypress Bark Roofing, and its Mukaehai is formed with a eave of Karahafū. It was relocated to its present site in {{ill|1873|en|1873|qid=Q7757|lt=1873 AD|1=1873}} (6th year of Meiji). * Hiraoka Shrine (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Osaka Prefecture) – Rebuilt in the 9th year of {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} ({{ill|1604|en|1604|qid=Q6738|lt=1604 AD|1=1604}}) by Toyotomi Hideyori with {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|en|Katagiri Katsumoto|qid=Q1882775|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}} as commissioner. The main hall is a one-kan building in the Kasuga-zukuri style with Cypress Bark Roofing. * Kotohira Shrine – Enshrines the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}}. * Itsukushima Shrine – Enshrines the deity {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}. * Inari Mounds – Numerous mounds enshrine the deity {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|qid=Q719665|lt=Inari Ōkami|1=Inari Ōkami}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall with Annex: 1 structure * Main Hall of the Three Shrines Subshrine * Main Hall of the Waterfall Shrine === Osaka Prefecture Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Hall of the Hiraoka Shrine === Important Artifacts === * Mikoshi – Possessing an inscribed ink name dated February 28, {{ill|1483|en|1483|qid=Q6637|lt=1483 AD|1=1483}} from the {{ill|Bunmei|en|Bunmei|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} era[http://www.city.osaka-izumi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/65/shiteibunkazai20190403.pdf Cultural Properties designated by Izumi City]. == 祭事 == * Annual Festival: {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|qid=Q2557|lt=May 10|1=May 10}} == Transportation == * Get off at {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} on the {{ill|Hanwa Line|en|Hanwa Line|qid=Q1154800|lt=Hanwa Line|1=Hanwa Line}} at {{ill|Kita-Shinoda Station|en|Kita-Shinoda Station|qid=Q856975|lt=Kitashinoda Station|1=Kita-Shinoda Station}}. == 周囲 == * {{ill|Kumano Kaidō|ja|熊野街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kaidō|1=Kumano Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11568976}} – The shrine’s {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=Torii Gate|1=Torii}} and {{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|qid=Q20155846|lt=Komainu|1=Komainu}} stand along the Kumano Kaidō. * {{ill|Shinoda Ōji|ja|篠田王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Ōji|1=Shinoda Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11603815}} * {{ill|Shinoda Mori Katsuwa Inari Shrine|ja|信太森葛葉稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoda Mori Katsuwa Inari Shrine|1=Shinodanomori Kuzunoha Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11386029}} * {{ill|Old Furu Shrine|ja|舊府神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Furu Shrine|1=Furufu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11613492}} (ふるふじんじゃ) – Enshrines a white fox “bakeishi.” * Shinoda no Mori Furusato-kan & Shinoda no Mori Kagamiike Historic Park – When walking from the shrine toward Kagamiike, one encounters “Nezumi-zaka,” a slope said to be where the father of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|qid=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}, {{ill|Abe no Yasuna|ja|安倍保名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yasuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, met his wife {{ill|Kuzunoha|en|Kuzunoha|qid=Q1651785|lt=Kuzunoha|1=Kuzunoha}}. * {{ill|Inryō-ji|ja|蔭凉寺 (和泉市)|lt=Inryō-ji|lt_ja=蔭凉寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inryō-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|en|Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|qid=Q11417846|lt=Izumi Koganezuka Kofun|1=Izumi Koganezuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Ikegami-Sone Site|en|Ikegami-Sone Site|qid=Q11551440|lt=Ikegami/Sone Ruins|1=Ikegami-Sone Site}} == Footnote == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Hijiri Shrine History == External links == *[https://izbun.jp/introduction/shinoda/hijirijinja.html Hijiri Shrine – Cultural Properties of Izumi City] *{{Commonscat-inline}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|聖神社|version=103539805|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:聖神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11611101}}
